step_by_step_reasoning: Based on the whiteboard and transcript, there are nine main components: Customer Applications (represented as UserCompanyAPI), Kinesis, Fargate, Aurora, S3, a Scheduler (EventBridge), EMR, EventBridge (for violations), and SQS. The ingestion flow goes from Applications through Kinesis and Fargate to Aurora and S3. The analytical flow is triggered by the Scheduler to Fargate, which spins up an EMR job reading from Aurora and S3, then sends violations to EventBridge, SQS, and back to Fargate for processing.

graph.name: Infor Governance Risk and Compliance Architecture
graph.notes: Ingests millions of transactions daily into Aurora and S3. Uses Fargate and EMR Spark to scope and execute rule audits, forwarding violations to EventBridge/SQS.

nodes.name: Applications
nodes.notes: Customer transactional and security source applications.

nodes.name: Fargate
nodes.notes: Parses ingestion data, manages scoping of analysis, and processes violations.

nodes.name: Aurora
nodes.notes: Stores parsed security and transaction logs.

nodes.name: S3
nodes.notes: Long-term data lake storage; transformed via Glue.

nodes.name: EMR
nodes.notes: Spark cluster executing audit rule analysis.

edges.notes: Pulls for decoration and customer alert SMTP.
